description: Fix end-to-end message delivery and add tests covering the full Slack → relay → CP → container → Slack response chain

userStories.title: Fix message-server: retry openclaw if response is empty (gateway startup race)
userStories.description: As a user, I want my first message to always get a response, even if the agent gateway is still initializing when the first message arrives.
acceptanceCriteria: message-server.js retries the openclaw command up to 3 times with 3-second delays if the response text is empty
If all retries return empty, message-server returns { ok: false, error: 'Agent returned empty response after retries' } with HTTP 500
On successful non-empty response, returns { ok: true, response: text } as before
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The race condition: container starts, message arrives within seconds, openclaw gateway is still initializing so --json returns empty payloads. Fix: wrap forwardToOpenclaw in a retry loop with 3s delay between attempts. The file is docker/tenant-image/message-server.js (plain JS, no TypeScript).

userStories.title: Fix relay: always send a Slack DM when message delivery fails or returns empty
userStories.description: As a user, I want to always receive some response in Slack — even if the agent fails, I should get an error message rather than silence.
acceptanceCriteria: In apps/slack-relay/src/app-factory.ts, when msgBody.ok is true but msgBody.response is empty/falsy AND msgBody.blocks is null, send a fallback DM: 'I received your message but had trouble generating a response. Please try again.'
When msgBody.ok is false (CP returned non-2xx), the existing error log is preserved and a DM is sent
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: File: apps/slack-relay/src/app-factory.ts around lines 281-295. Add an else clause after the existing msgBody.response check to send a fallback message when response is empty.

userStories.title: Add e2e test: full message delivery chain with real message-server
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an e2e test that exercises the full message delivery path from relay → CP → message-server mock → relay → Slack DM.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file: tests/e2e/message-delivery.test.ts
Test starts a real relay + real control plane (mocked docker)
Test provisions a tenant and sets it ACTIVE
Test sets up a mock message server (HTTP server on a random port) that returns { ok: true, response: 'Hello from agent' }
Test updates the tenant's container to point to the mock message server (or mock the fetch call to the container)
Test sends a Slack event POST /slack/events to the relay
Test asserts that chat.postMessage was called with the correct channel and text 'Hello from agent'
Test asserts the message_queue row ends up as DELIVERED
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Pattern: similar to tests/integration/relay-to-cp-contract.test.ts but full e2e. Use the vi.fn() mock for fetchFn to intercept Slack API calls. The tricky part is making the CP forward to a mock container endpoint — look at how the CP /message endpoint calls the container (uses fetch to http://containerName:3100/message, where containerName is derived from tenantId). You can intercept this by mocking globalThis.fetch in the CP, or by using a real HTTP mock server and patching the container hostname resolution.

userStories.title: Commit, push and create PR
userStories.description: All fixes and tests committed, pushed and PR opened.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm test passes (81+ test files)
Changes committed
Branch pushed to origin
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
